item.title: Los Angeles Warehouse Fire Smoke Chokes Southern California for Fifth Day
item.description: A fire at a cold storage warehouse near downtown Los Angeles continued pumping fumes across Southern California five days after it ignited, prompting emergency declarations as air quality reached unhealthy levels.

item.title: What Starmer’s Resignation Means for the UK’s Climate Goals
item.description: The government has faced growing pressure to loosen its commitment to cutting emissions.

item.title: Heat Wave Shows Why French Reactors Must Adapt to Climate Change
item.description: Global warming and extreme weather are forcing French nuclear reactors to adapt.

item.title: UK’s World-Leading Climate Goals Look Shakier in Post-Starmer Era
item.description: The British government faces significant pressure to make policy concessions on fossil fuels. It has stood firm, so far.

item.title: Twelve EU Nations Push to Boost Carbon Fund for Poorer States
item.description: A group of 12 European Union countries urged the bloc’s executive to bolster a fund financed through the carbon market which supports poorer member states in the transition away from fossil fuels.

item.title: Octopus Energy, CATL Announce Battery-Swapping Hubs for Trucks
item.description: UK energy supplier Octopus Energy Group Ltd. and Chinese battery manufacturer Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., or CATL, will build a European network of battery-swapping hubs for trucks, the two companies said on Monday.

item.title: What Climate Change Costs You at the Checkout
item.description: The higher temperatures climb, the more inflated household costs like groceries will become, economists predict.

item.title: Red Weather Warning Issued in UK for Extreme Heat and Humidity
item.description: Red weather warning issued for extreme heat and humidityBy Emily Beament and Rebecca Speare-Cole, Press Association

item.title: Climate Fund Guarantees Set to Woo $5 Billion for Mexico, Brazil
item.description: The Climate Investment Funds, a $14 billion pool of climate-focused capital, said it has approved funding for Brazil and Mexico that should help unlock billions of dollars in additional financing to speed up the decarbonization of their industrial sectors.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Intensifies With France and UK on Red Alert
item.description: A heat wave gripping western Europe is forecast to strengthen this week, with days of dangerously hot and humid conditions and record-breaking temperatures in France, the UK, Spain and Germany.

item.title: England’s Critical Infrastructure Upgrades Bring Steep Water Bills
item.description: The costs of overhauling climate-stressed water systems are being passed along to customers.

item.title: Harsher Weather Is Coming for India’s Health Insurance Bills
item.description: Insurance companies are selling new products as climate change and pollution make people sick.

item.title: What to Watch at London Climate Week
item.description: The city is hosting CEOs, policymakers and activists to discuss some of the most pressing climate issues.

item.title: Driving an EV in These US States Saves You the Most Money
item.description: Some of the places with the most expensive gas have the cheapest electricity, including Arizona, Utah and Washington.

item.title: BYD Rejects Claims It Violated Hungary’s Environmental Rules
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD Co. said it rejected allegations it breached environmental rules in Hungary during the construction of its first plant in the European Union.

item.title: Spain Weighs Financial Aid to Struggling Solar Power Industry
item.description: The Spanish government is weighing whether to offer financial support to a domestic solar industry that’s grappling with oversupply and negative wholesale power prices.

item.title: California and US West Threatened by Wildfires Over Coming Days
item.description: The fire threat across the US West will rise Friday and remain dangerous through Saturday for many areas, including Northern California, as lightning strikes risk igniting dry vegetation.

item.title: European Firefighters Call for More Resources as More Wildfires Loom
item.description: They’re bracing for another severe fire season.

item.title: Firefighters Warn They’re Ill-Prepared for a Bad Wildfire Season
item.description: With another hot summer looming, fire services across Europe face funding and labor shortfalls.

item.title: Record Heat Wave Sears France With Farms and Power Grids Tested
item.description: Western Europe’s heat wave is forecast to intensify this weekend, putting France on track for record temperatures and testing power grids as demand for air conditioning surges.

item.title: US Regulator Makes ‘Interventionist’ Pivot to Speed Data Centers
item.description: The chairman of the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission said moves to help AI data centers accelerate hook ups to the country’s electric grids reflect a new and more interventionist approach by the regulator.

item.title: Trump Administration Backtracks on Removing Ocean Sensors
item.description: The U-turn comes after a plan to dismantle the monitors sparked opposition from scientists and members of Congress.

item.title: Over 100 Countries Back UN Framework for Climate Migration
item.description: More than 100 countries endorsed a new United Nations framework for communities so threatened by climate change that they may need to resettle elsewhere.

item.title: Climate Negotiators Reach Finance Sticking Point in Bonn Talks
item.description: Representatives at the United Nations talks couldn’t agree on how to pay to combat global warming.

item.title: Finance Fight Clouds UN Climate Talks Even as War Adds Momentum
item.description: Negotiators in Bonn got stuck on a familiar problem: how to find money to help developing nations tackle global warming.

item.title: US Eases Environmental Reviews for Natural Gas Projects
item.description: A top US energy regulator voted to ease environmental requirements for pipelines and other natural gas projects, saying they’re not needed after a recent Supreme Court ruling.

item.title: UK Government Eases Pressure on Tougher Green Building Upgrades
item.description: The UK has delayed rules that will require commercial landlords to meet stricter energy efficiency regulations for some of their buildings, giving building owners five years to meet the new standards while exempting smaller properties.

item.title: Pimco Targets Out-of-Date Assets in New Real Estate Strategy
item.description: Pimco Prime Real Estate is rolling out a new strategy that will target out-of-date buildings in major cities, with a view to making them more suited to a world increasingly shaped by extreme weather.

item.title: US Northeast Threatened by Severe Thunderstorms, Damaging Winds
item.description: More than 63 million Americans in a Northeast Corridor that spans Washington, through New York, to Boston are threatened by wild weather on Thursday, including severe thunderstorms and damaging winds.

item.title: US West Set to Sizzle This Summer While Northeast Stays Cooler
item.description: The US West and Gulf Coast are set to broil this summer while the rest of the nation may face dreary and cool days — thanks in part to El Niño.
